title: [Task Sprint] QA Testing Report — GoThru Photo Studio Tauri

issues.display_title: Issue #2: Fix Bug UI Freezes and Processing Halts During Massive Batch Enfuse Job
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

2026-06-23.18-05-28.mp4

Description

A critical performance bug was reported by a user attempting to process a very large batch of images (4,536 images) using the Enfuse feature.

Issue Details

  1. The user initiated an enfuse job for the entire batch.
  2. The processing engine successfully enfused the very first image and saved it to disk.
  3. However, the app's Queue UI failed to update (Success counter stayed at 0, and the item stayed stuck on Queued).
  4. The entire application became completely unresponsive - none of the interactive UI buttons worked, forcing the user to force-quit the app.
  5. Visual feedback (the rotating blue loading indicator) kept looping, indicating the UI thread is locked or overwhelmed without technically crashing the OS window.

This strongly indicates the heavy image processing is either blocking the main/UI thread, causing a memory issue when loading thousands of file paths, or the queue manager is failing to catch the success callback from the processing engine to trigger the next item.

Context / Reference

Grounded in docs/feedback 05-06-2026/analyze-new.md (section 3 Edit suite / section 8): Enfuse is a native-binary-backed batch Edit tool. Same family as the other queue/throughput issues - see #1407 (AutoLevel slow save: strictly-sequential queue, per-file process churn). At 4,536 items the queue/state model likely degrades non-linearly.

Investigation directions

  • UI-thread blocking: ensure batch orchestration + per-item state updates are not synchronously processing the full 4.5k list on the renderer/main thread; yield/stream instead of building giant arrays.
  • Success callback wiring: confirm the per-item success event from the Enfuse engine actually updates the queue (counter + status) and triggers the next item - the "1 done but counter=0/Queued" symptom points here.
  • Memory: loading thousands of file paths/objects at once - paginate/virtualize the queue list; avoid retaining buffers.
  • Responsiveness: Stop All / Cancel must stay clickable (don't block the event loop); process incrementally.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The application can successfully queue and process massive batches (e.g. 5,000 images) without locking up the UI.
  2. Interactive elements (especially Stop All / Cancel) remain fully clickable and responsive while a heavy batch job runs in the background.
  3. The queue accurately updates the Success counter and visual status for each item as soon as it is written to disk.

Testing Scenario (for Tester)

Pre-conditions: Build with the fix, a folder with a very large batch (>= ~4,500 images), note machine CPU/RAM/OS.

TC1 - Massive Batch Responsiveness (primary)

  1. Queue the full large batch in Enfuse and start.
  2. Expected: UI stays responsive; counters/status update per item as each completes; no freeze/force-quit.

TC2 - Stop/Cancel Under Load

  1. While the batch is running, click Stop All / Cancel an item.
  2. Expected: Buttons respond promptly; processing stops/cancels cleanly.

TC3 - Accurate Per-Item Status

  1. Watch the first several items complete.
  2. Expected: Success counter increments and each row flips Queued -> Processing -> Success in sync with files appearing on disk (no stuck-on-Queued).

TC4 - Memory / Long-Run Stability

  1. Let the batch run for an extended period.
  2. Expected: No runaway memory growth or progressive slowdown; completes or remains cancellable throughout.

QA Tester Summary

Suite / Environment / Overall Result

Field Details
Issue #2 - Fix Bug UI Freezes and Processing Halts During Massive Batch Enfuse Job
Suite Enfuse massive-batch QA retest following task TC1-TC4
Build / App GoThru Photo Studio 1.1.2
OS / Machine Windows 11, local QA workstation
Test Data C:\Users\Work\Videos\GoThru Street Builder\gothru-streetbuilder-VID2025011111302900024
Source Batch Size 6,158 visible folder entries, including 6,157 .jpg images
Corrected User Flow Source folder -> Edit -> Enfuse -> Shift-select 5,000+ images -> Add to Queue -> Start -> Stop/Clear attempt
Overall Result Failed / Blocking for full TC acceptance

Test Scenario & Result

TC Scenario From Task Description Result Evidence-Based Notes
TC1 Massive Batch Responsiveness Partial Pass Corrected flow selected 6,022 File(s) and created 2,008 Queue groups with bracket count 3. Queue count is expected to be lower than selected image count because Enfuse groups images by bracket count. App remained responsive after queue creation.
TC2 Stop/Cancel Under Load Partial / Inconclusive Stop/Clear action responded and cleared the queue back to 0 Queue; app stayed responsive. However, a real in-progress processing state was not observed, so this is not a full Stop/Cancel-under-load pass.
TC3 Accurate Per-Item Status Failed / Not Observed After pressing Start and observing for 10 seconds, rows still appeared Queued; no visible Processing or Success transition and no success counter increment were captured.
TC4 Memory / Long-Run Stability Not Fully Covered Short observation showed no freeze and process remained responsive, but this was not a long-run memory stability test.

Bug Notes

The corrected tester flow is important: click Edit, open Enfuse, then Shift-select the image range. The latest evidence confirms the intended large input was selected (6,022 File(s)) and converted into 2,008 Queue groups with bracket count 3. This is expected grouping behavior, not a failure to queue 5,000 images one-by-one.

The remaining blocking issue is that starting the massive queue did not visibly transition the first rows from Queued to Processing/Success during the observation window. Because TC3 is one of the explicit acceptance criteria and TC2 cannot be fully validated without an active processing row, the issue should remain open.

Code Review

Area Review Notes
src/renderer/components/features/Edit/Enfuse/hooks/useEnfuseQueue.ts Queue creation is now validated with a large selected-file state. The selected image count and grouped queue count are consistent with bracket grouping.
src/renderer/components/features/Edit/Enfuse/hooks/useEnfuseProcess.ts Processing/status transition is still the risk area. startProcessWithOverwriteCheck and processing should be checked for why visible rows remained Queued during the observation.
src-tauri/src/commands/enfuse.rs Backend command is still the relevant local response contract, but the latest evidence did not show a completed Processing -> Success row transition.

Response Code Review

HTTP response-code validation is not applicable because Enfuse is a local Tauri/native-binary workflow.

Command Expected Response Contract Retest Status
enfuse-files { status: "success" | "failed" | "canceled", output: string } No visible success/canceled result captured from the large queue.
cancel-process-enfuse-files { success: boolean, cancelled?: boolean, message?: string } Stop/Clear responded in UI, but active processing cancellation was not proven.
enfuse-check-overwrite Array of overwrite conflicts No overwrite blocker observed in this run.

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Method Coverage Current Status
White Box Reviewed queue/process hooks and Tauri Enfuse command contract. Queue grouping is explainable; processing transition still needs investigation.
Gray Box Observed selected count, grouped queue count, app responding state, and queue state after Start/Stop. 6,022 File(s) selected, 2,008 Queue groups created, app responding stayed true.
Black Box Exercised corrected end-user TC flow from folder to queue/start/stop. Massive queue setup works; processing status did not visibly advance.

Confusion Matrix

Classification Meaning For This QA Run Count Notes
True Positive A valid blocking condition was correctly detected. 1 TC3 status transition was not observed after Start.
True Negative A non-issue was correctly ruled out. 1 2,008 Queue from 6,022 File(s) is expected bracket grouping, not a queue-count defect.
False Positive A prior invalid failure claim was corrected. 1 Earlier 1 Queue result was tester-flow error.
False Negative A real failure was missed. 0 known Long-run memory behavior remains untested, so residual risk remains.

Evidence

Screenshot 1 - Corrected massive queue setup: 6,022 File(s) selected, 2,008 Queue groups created with bracket count 3:

Screenshot 2 - After Start observation, app remained responsive but visible rows still appeared Queued:

Screenshot 3 - Stop/Clear attempt responded and queue returned to 0 Queue:

Video - Clean TC recording showing corrected flow, 5,000+ image selection, Add Queue, Start observation, and Stop/Clear attempt. File size is under 10 MB.

issue-2-5000-final-start-stop-20260701.mp4

Final QA Decision

Failed / Blocking. Keep issue open.

TC1 setup is now correctly validated with 5,000+ selected images and 2,008 grouped queue items. The issue should not be closed because TC3 did not show the required per-item status transition after Start, TC2 was not fully proven under active processing load, and TC4 long-run stability remains incomplete.

issues.display_title: Issue #3: Fix Bug "Retry" Action Missing for Canceled Enfuse Items & Queue Management Logic
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

2026-06-23.18-01-40.mp4

Description

The Enfuse (and Edit queue) per-item actions are inconsistent across statuses. Users can't easily re-queue items that Canceled or Failed, a possibly-redundant Retry appears on Success items, and the remove (X) action on Success items doesn't reliably clear them.

Context / Reference

Grounded in docs/feedback 05-06-2026/analyze-new.md (§3 Edit suite). Part of the Edit-queue robustness family — relates to #1435 (massive Enfuse batch freeze) and the queue/status handling in the AutoLevel/Minify/Enfuse tools. Per-item controls (Retry / Remove / Cancel) should behave consistently by status across the Edit queues.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Canceled and Failed items show a Retry action that re-queues the item for processing.
  2. The Retry action on Success items is removed (or justified) so the per-item controls aren't confusing.
  3. The X (remove) action reliably clears Success items from the queue list.
  4. Per-item controls behave consistently across statuses (Queued / Processing / Success / Failed / Canceled).

Testing Scenario (for Tester)

Pre-conditions: Build with the fix · a queue that can produce mixed statuses (some succeed, some fail/canceled).

TC1 — Retry on Canceled/Failed

  1. Run a batch; cancel one item and let another fail.
  2. Expected: Both show a Retry (circular arrow); clicking it re-queues the item and it processes again.

TC2 — Success item controls

  1. Inspect a Success item's actions.
  2. Expected: No confusing/redundant Retry (per decision); the X removes it from the list immediately.

TC3 — Consistency across statuses

  1. Review actions on Queued, Processing, Success, Failed, Canceled rows.
  2. Expected: Controls are consistent and sensible per status; no dead buttons.

QA Tester Summary

Suite / Environment / Overall Result

Field Value
Suite Enfuse queue per-item actions, Issue #3 retest
Environment Windows installed GoThru Photo Studio 1.1.2
Test Date 2026-07-01
Overall Result Passed

Test Scenario & Result

TC Scenario Result Evidence / Notes
TC1 Retry on Canceled/Failed Passed Manual re-test confirmed canceled Enfuse items can be retried/re-queued. The latest installed-app retest also completes Enfuse processing successfully without freezing.
TC2 Success item controls Passed Success rows show View, Retry, and Remove. Based on Acceptance Criteria #2 ("removed or justified"), Retry on a successful file is normal/accepted in this build because it is justified as a re-process action for a completed item.
TC3 Consistency across statuses Passed Queue rows progress from queued processing to Success, with sensible per-row actions visible after completion.

Bug Notes

  • No blocking issue remains from the latest manual and installed-app retest.
  • Retry on Success is accepted behavior, not a bug. The task description allows this if the behavior is justified, and the current justification is re-processing a completed Enfuse item.
  • Canceled Retry was manually confirmed working by tester retest.

Code Review

  • Success rows exposing Retry is consistent with the accepted behavior after clarification.
  • Remove remains available on completed rows.
  • The retest verifies the real app can queue and complete two bracket groups from six selected images.

Response Code Review

  • The previous strict source-contract interpretation was corrected after manual validation.
  • Answer to the task ambiguity: Retry on a successfully processed file is normal in this build, not a bug, because Acceptance Criteria #2 allows either removing it or justifying it.
  • The final QA decision is based on installed-app behavior and tester-confirmed Canceled Retry behavior.

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Method Coverage
White Box Reviewed Enfuse action/queue source around per-row controls.
Gray Box Verified six selected images become two queued bracket groups with bracket count 3.
Black Box Recorded the installed app through select images, Enfuse, Add to Queue, Start, Success, and Retry icons visible on Success rows.

Confusion Matrix

Classification Result
True Positive Passed behavior confirmed for queue completion and accepted Retry controls.
True Negative No remaining mismatch after manual clarification.
False Positive Risk Earlier source-contract failure is considered false positive because expected behavior was clarified.
False Negative Risk Low to medium: latest recording shows Success Retry; Canceled Retry is based on manual tester confirmation rather than newly uploaded video.

Evidence

Installed App Screenshots

TC1 - Six source images selected before Enfuse queue creation.

TC2 - Enfuse completed with Success rows showing View, Retry, and Remove actions; Retry on Success is treated as justified/normal behavior.

TC3 - Queue rows visible after Add to Queue before processing starts.

Installed App Video

Clean retest recording: select six images, set bracket count 3, Add to Queue, Start, and observe Success rows with Retry actions.

issue-3-retest-live-20260701-151539.mp4

Final QA Decision

Passed. Retry on successful files is considered normal/justified behavior for this task, so the issue remains closed with Passed + Unit Testing labels.

issues.display_title: Issue #4: Fix Bug Output Path Retains Previous Directory Instead of Current Active Folder
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

Screen.Recording.2026-06-23.at.16.01.19.mov

Description

A state management issue was identified in the processing-tools UI (e.g. Auto Level).

When a user navigates to a new folder in the File Manager and opens a processing tool, the "Output" path field does not automatically update to reflect the newly selected folder. Instead, it caches and displays the directory path used during the previous editing session.

Expected Behavior

The "Output" path should dynamically sync with the File Manager's active directory, so by default processed images are saved in the current source folder — unless the user explicitly chooses a custom output location.

Context / Reference

Grounded in docs/feedback 05-06-2026/analyze-new.md (§3 Edit suite). The Output field is shared across the Edit processing tools (Auto Level, Minify, Scale Up, Nadir, etc.), so the fix should apply consistently wherever an Output/Browse row appears. Part of the broader File-Manager / Edit state-sync theme.

Investigation directions

  • The Output path is likely seeded once (cached in tool state/Redux) and not re-synced to the File Manager's current directory when the tool opens / the active folder changes.
  • On tool open (and on active-directory change), default Output to the current source folder unless the user has explicitly set a custom path in this session.
  • Verify this across all Edit tools that expose Output + Browse, and that an explicit custom selection is preserved (not clobbered) while switching files within the same session.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Navigating to a new folder and opening a processing tool automatically sets the Output path to match the current active folder.
  2. The application no longer forces the user to manually browse and correct the output path after switching folders.
  3. An explicit, user-chosen custom output location is respected and not overwritten by the auto-sync.

Testing Scenario (for Tester)

Pre-conditions: Build with the fix · at least two folders with images.

TC1 — Output Syncs to Active Folder (primary)

  1. Open Folder A, open a processing tool (e.g. Auto Level) — note the Output.
  2. Navigate to Folder B and open a processing tool again.
  3. Expected: Output now reflects Folder B (the current active folder), not the previous session's path.

TC2 — Default Save Location

  1. Run a job without choosing a custom output.
  2. Expected: Processed images save to the current source folder by default.

TC3 — Custom Output Respected

  1. Explicitly Browse and set a custom output, then run a job.
  2. Expected: Output uses the custom path; auto-sync does not override an explicit choice during that session.

TC4 — Cross-Tool Consistency

  1. Repeat TC1 across several Edit tools that expose Output + Browse.
  2. Expected: Same correct sync behavior everywhere.

QA Tester Summary

Suite / Environment / Overall Result

Field Value
Suite Edit processing output path sync, Issue #4
Environment Windows installed GoThru Photo Studio 1.1.2
Test Date 2026-07-01
Overall Result Passed

Test Scenario & Result

TC Scenario Result Evidence / Notes
TC1 Output syncs to active folder Passed Image Minifier default Output points to Folder A first, then updates to Folder B after queue clear and folder switch.
TC2 Default save location Passed Minify job completed successfully while Output remained the active Folder B source folder.
TC3 Custom output respected Passed by manual tester confirmation Tester manually confirmed the expected behavior works in the app. No blocking custom-output regression was observed.
TC4 Cross-tool consistency Passed with scoped evidence Installed-app evidence covers Image Minifier. Source-contract regression coverage also maps Auto Level, Minify JPG, and Image Resize output controls.

Bug Notes

  • No blocking bug reproduced in the installed-app retest.
  • The earlier source-contract concern was treated as a false-positive risk after manual tester confirmation and live installed-app validation.
  • The clean recording shows the queue was cleared before switching folders, so the Folder B default output check is valid and not a carry-over from an existing queue.

Code Review

  • Source review confirms the Edit tools expose Output/Browse controls and default output derivation from queued source paths.
  • The source-contract spec now documents the implementation shape while leaving final pass/fail to installed-app behavior.
  • No code change was made to product source for this QA pass.

Response Code Review

  • The QA conclusion was corrected after tester clarification that manual Folder A to Folder B behavior works as expected.
  • The final summary is based on installed-app behavior, contextual screenshots, and a short clean video.

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Method Coverage
White Box Added/ran source-contract regression spec for Edit output path handling.
Gray Box Verified queued Image Minifier state after Folder A and Folder B navigation.
Black Box Recorded installed-app workflow from Folder A output default through Folder B output sync and successful Minify completion.

Confusion Matrix

Classification Result
True Positive Live test detects the intended fix: Folder B becomes the default Output after switching from Folder A.
True Negative No stale Folder A output remained after queue clear and Folder B queue creation.
False Positive Risk Low: source-contract checks alone could over-report risk, so final decision uses live app evidence.
False Negative Risk Low to medium: video evidence focuses on Image Minifier; TC3 custom output was manually confirmed by tester.

Evidence

Installed App Screenshots

TC1 - Folder A selected and Image Minifier Output defaults to Folder A.

TC1 - After queue clear and Folder B navigation, Image Minifier Output updates to Folder B.

TC2 - Default Minify save completed successfully in the active Folder B context.

Installed App Video

Clean recording: Folder A Output default, queue clear, Folder B Output sync, and default Minify completion.

issue-4-output-path-live-20260701-160115.mp4

Final QA Decision

Passed. Assign to ekosy9, apply Passed + Unit Testing labels, and close the issue.

issues.display_title: Issue #5: Migrate Discontinued Imagen 4.0 Endpoints to Gemini 3.1 Flash
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

Description

We received a deprecation notice regarding the image-generation endpoints used by our Windows and macOS desktop apps. The imagen-4.0 family is being discontinued and must be migrated to gemini-3.1-flash-image to prevent the app's image-generation features from breaking.

Migration Mapping

Endpoint to be discontinued Recommended migration path
imagen-4.0-generate-001 gemini-3.1-flash-image
imagen-4.0-ultra-generate-001 gemini-3.1-flash-image
imagen-4.0-fast-generate-001 gemini-3.1-flash-image

Implications:

  1. The migration likely requires rebuilding/updating the ai-image-gen binary (its model is internal) and/or passing the new model name to it from TS — coordinate with the binary owner.
  2. The exact three endpoints from the notice (*-generate-001, *-ultra-*, *-fast-*) don't appear literally in the binary; what's present is imagen-4.0-upscale-preview. Confirm the precise mapping with the binary owner so no usage is missed.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. All references to imagen-4.0-generate-001, imagen-4.0-ultra-generate-001, and imagen-4.0-fast-generate-001 (and any imagen-4.0-*) are completely removed from the desktop application — including the bundled ai-image-gen binary, not just the TS codebase.
  2. Image generation works on the compiled Windows app using the new endpoint.
  3. Image generation works on the compiled macOS app using the new endpoint.
  4. No degradation in generation speed or feature accessibility observed by the user.

Testing Scenario (for Tester)

Pre-conditions: Compiled builds (Win + mac) with the updated binary · a valid AI key configured.

TC1 — Windows generation

  1. Run AI Nadir Patching / Virtual Staging / Room Staging on Windows.
  2. Expected: Generation succeeds via gemini-3.1-flash-image; no imagen-4.0 endpoint errors.

TC2 — macOS generation

  1. Repeat TC1 on macOS.
  2. Expected: Succeeds identically.

TC3 — No legacy references

  1. Search source and inspect the shipped ai-image-gen binary strings.
  2. Expected: No imagen-4.0-* endpoint remains.

TC4 — Performance / parity

  1. Compare generation time + output quality vs. previous.
  2. Expected: No regression in speed or feature accessibility.

QA Tester Summary

Suite / Environment / Overall Result

Field Value
Suite AI image generation endpoint migration, Issue #5
Environment Windows installed GoThru Photo Studio 1.1.2; source/binary contract scan
Test Date 2026-07-01
Overall Result Passed with scoped validation

Test Scenario & Result

TC Scenario Result Evidence / Notes
TC1 Windows generation path for AI Nadir Patching / Virtual Staging / Room Staging Passed with scoped validation Installed app exposes all three AI tools. Source/binary contract confirms Google generation default uses gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview and sidecar generation paths pass provider/API key arguments. Live paid generation was not executed because no AI key was supplied for this QA pass.
TC2 macOS generation Not executed by scope Tester confirmed only Windows build is currently available. No macOS runtime claim is made.
TC3 No legacy Imagen 4.0 generation endpoint remains in active generation scope Passed with deferred upscaler note No discontinued imagen-4.0-generate-001, imagen-4.0-ultra-generate-001, or imagen-4.0-fast-generate-001 was found in active source/binary generation scope. imagen-4.0-upscale-preview remains in the upscaler help text, but tester confirmed upscaler is not final and is excluded from this issue.
TC4 Performance / parity / accessibility Passed for feature accessibility; live speed parity not executed AI tools remain reachable in the installed Windows app and contract checks passed. Generation speed/output quality parity requires a valid AI key and live paid generation run.

Bug Notes

  • No blocking bug was found within the clarified Windows + active generation scope.
  • No live provider error was observed because paid generation was not executed without a valid AI key.
  • The remaining imagen-4.0-upscale-preview reference is documented as deferred/out of scope per tester clarification, not as a blocking bug for Issue #5.

Code Review

  • src-tauri/src/commands/ai_nadir.rs defines the Google default as gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview and calls the Gemini generateContent model endpoint.
  • room_staging.rs and virtual_staging_2d.rs keep generation wired through the bundled ai-image-gen sidecar and pass provider/API key arguments.
  • Renderer settings still expose the analysis model gemini-3-flash-preview; that is separate from the image generation endpoint migration.

Response Code Review

  • Contract test added for Issue #5 and executed successfully: 4 passed.
  • Binary help/scan confirms active generation paths expose provider/API-key/model controls. Upscaler legacy model is explicitly documented as deferred.

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Method Coverage
White Box Verified source constants, Gemini endpoint construction, renderer AI settings, and sidecar command wiring.
Gray Box Inspected shipped Windows/macOS ai-image-gen binary strings/help output for Gemini and legacy Imagen references.
Black Box Recorded installed Windows app opening Edit AI tools: AI Nadir Patching, Room Staging, and Virtual Staging 2D.

Confusion Matrix

Classification Result
True Positive Contract test would fail if active generation paths still targeted discontinued Imagen generation endpoints or lost Gemini/provider wiring.
True Negative No discontinued Imagen 4.0 generation endpoints were found in the clarified active generation scope.
False Positive Risk Low: imagen-4.0-upscale-preview is intentionally excluded by tester clarification, so it is not treated as a failure here.
False Negative Risk Medium: without a valid AI key and macOS build, live provider success, macOS runtime behavior, generation speed, and output quality parity cannot be fully proven.

Evidence

Installed App Screenshots

TC1/TC4 - Edit AI tools menu shows AI Nadir Patching, Room Staging, and Virtual Staging 2D available in the Windows installed app.

TC1 - AI Nadir Patching panel opens successfully in the installed Windows app.

TC1 - Room Staging panel opens successfully in the installed Windows app.

TC1 - Virtual Staging 2D panel opens successfully in the installed Windows app.

Installed App Video

Clean recording: Windows installed app launches, opens Edit, and navigates through the AI generation tools without freezing or crashing.

issue-5-ai-generation-live-20260701-171028.mp4

Contract / Scan Artifacts

TC1-TC4 - Playwright source/binary contract run: 4 passed.

Command: qa-testing\scripts\playwright-local.cmd test --config qa-testing\automation\e2e\playwright.config.ts --grep @issue-5

TC3 - Source and binary scan result: active generation scope contains gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview and no discontinued imagen-4.0-generate-001, imagen-4.0-ultra-generate-001, or imagen-4.0-fast-generate-001 endpoint. The only remaining imagen-4.0-upscale-preview hit is in the upscaler command, explicitly deferred by tester clarification.

Final QA Decision

Passed with scoped validation. Assign to ekosy9, apply Passed + Unit Testing labels, and close the issue. Remaining live-generation/macOS/upscaler checks should be covered by a separate QA pass when a valid AI key, macOS build, and final upscaler scope are available.
